stimulus: Using salvaged municipal materials, such as cracked ______blank such as fishing nets; and conventional craft materials, such as cedar bark, Coast Salish weaver Mira Quinault assembles her large-format hanging panels, which she hopes will prompt urban viewers to reconsider how waste circulates through their watersheds.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The series is composed of three complex items, each followed by "such as…," containing internal commas. Semicolons therefore separate the items, while a comma sets off the introductory "such as fishing nets" inside the middle item.

Choice B is incorrect because using commas between items that themselves contain commas breaks the parallel separation. Choice C is incorrect because a colon would wrongly introduce only one item rather than continue a three-item series. Choice D is incorrect because the second semicolon (after "marine refuse") prevents the inner comma pattern from setting off "such as fishing nets."
